content_text:
Morning traffic is picking up again as the school year starts, and you’re already feeling the squeeze between dropping the kids off and getting to work on time. You glance at the clock, realize you’re running late, and decide to skip the usual pre-drive routine. As you pull out of the driveway, you’re still mentally reviewing your to-do list and barely notice the school bus pulling out in front of you. You slam on the brakes just in time, your heart racing.

This moment highlights how the rush and distraction of back-to-school mornings can cloud your focus and put you — and others — at risk. Taking a brief pause to complete a Rate Your State (RYS) check before starting your engine can help you reset and reconnect, ensuring your eyes and mind are fully on the task ahead.
image_prompt: A suburban street at dawn, the sky just beginning to lighten. A car is pulling out of a driveway, the driver distracted by their phone and barely noticing the school bus pulling out in front of them. The driver's eyes widen in surprise as they slam on the brakes just in time to avoid a collision. The scene is captured from a low angle, emphasizing the driver's startled expression and the close proximity of the school bus. The environment is quiet, with dew still on the grass and the soft glow of early morning light. The car and bus are both in sharp focus, while the background is slightly blurred to draw attention to the main action.
summary: This image depicts a scenario where a driver narrowly avoids a collision with a school bus due to morning distractions, emphasizing the importance of staying focused and aware while driving.
alt_text: A driver narrowly avoids a collision with a school bus by slamming on the brakes just in time.
